title: Apostles' Creed
desc: The oldest summary of the Christian faith, used universally in worship and catechesis.

title: Nicene Creed
desc: The ecumenical creed defining Trinitarian orthodoxy, formulated at Nicaea and Constantinople.

title: Athanasian Creed
desc: A detailed exposition of the Trinity and the two natures of Christ.

title: Council of Nicaea I
desc: The first ecumenical council (325) — defined Christ as homoousios (consubstantial) with the Father and condemned Arianism.

title: Council of Constantinople I
desc: The second ecumenical council (381) — completed the Nicene Creed and affirmed the full deity of the Holy Spirit against the Pneumatomachi.

title: Council of Ephesus
desc: The third ecumenical council (431) — affirmed Mary as Theotokos and condemned Nestorianism; the Twelve Anathemas of Cyril.

title: Council of Chalcedon
desc: The fourth ecumenical council (451) — defined Christ as one Person in two natures, inconfusedly, unchangeably, indivisibly, inseparably.

title: Council of Orange II
desc: A western provincial council (529) — affirmed sovereign grace and condemned Semi-Pelagianism; foundational for Augustinian soteriology.

title: Augsburg Confession
author: Philip Melanchthon
desc: 28 articles presented to Emperor Charles V — the founding document of Lutheran theology.

title: Belgic Confession
author: Guido de Brès
desc: 37 articles affirming Reformed doctrine, written by Guido de Brès against Rome and Anabaptism.

title: Heidelberg Catechism
author: Zacharias Ursinus
desc: 129 Q&As organized around comfort in life and death, structured in 52 Lord's Days.

title: Thirty-Nine Articles
desc: Doctrinal standard of the Church of England, combining Reformed theology with episcopal polity.

title: Canons of Dort
desc: Five heads of doctrine affirming particular grace against the Arminian Remonstrance (TULIP).

title: Westminster Confession of Faith
desc: 33 chapters of systematic Reformed theology — the most comprehensive English-language confession.

title: Westminster Shorter Catechism
desc: 107 Q&As for catechesis, opening with "What is the chief end of man?"

title: Westminster Larger Catechism
desc: 196 Q&As with full exposition of the Ten Commandments and Lord's Prayer.

title: Second London Baptist Confession
desc: 32 chapters following the WCF, adapted for Particular Baptist doctrine of believers' baptism.

title: Letter to the Ephesians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: On unity with the bishop, faith and love as the beginning and end of life, and the medicine of immortality.

title: Letter to the Magnesians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: Against Judaizing; on respecting a youthful bishop; living on the Lord's Day not the Sabbath.

title: Letter to the Trallians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: On obedience to bishop and deacons; against Docetism — Christ truly suffered and rose.

title: Justin Martyr — Overview
author: Justin Martyr
desc: Philosopher-apologist on the Logos, fulfilment of prophecy, and Christian worship.

title: First Apology
author: Justin Martyr
desc: Addressed to Emperor Antoninus Pius — defence of Christians, the Logos doctrine, baptism, and the Eucharist.

title: Second Apology
author: Justin Martyr
desc: A brief supplement responding to three recent executions and defending Christian philosophy against Crescens.

title: Irenaeus of Lyons — Overview
author: Irenaeus of Lyons
desc: On the Rule of Faith, apostolic tradition, and recapitulation — Christ as the new Adam.

title: Tertullian — Overview
author: Tertullian
desc: First major Latin theologian; coined trinitas and established Trinitarian vocabulary.

title: Athanasius of Alexandria — Overview
author: Athanasius of Alexandria
desc: Champion of Nicene orthodoxy; on the Incarnation and salvation by union with Christ.

title: Gregory of Nazianzus — Overview
author: Gregory of Nazianzus
desc: "The Theologian" on the Trinity, the full deity of the Spirit, and Christ's two natures.

title: John Chrysostom — Overview
author: John Chrysostom
desc: The greatest ancient preacher on Scripture, grace, prayer, and care for the poor.

title: On the Priesthood
author: John Chrysostom
desc: Six books on the high calling and terrible weight of pastoral ministry — why Chrysostom fled ordination.

title: Augustine of Hippo — Overview
author: Augustine of Hippo
desc: On the restless heart, sovereign grace, original sin, the Trinity, and the two cities.

title: Confessions
author: Augustine of Hippo
desc: "Our heart is restless until it rests in Thee" — autobiography, prayer, and meditation on grace across 13 books.

title: Ninety-Five Theses
author: Martin Luther
desc: The 95 propositions challenging indulgences that sparked the Reformation — posted at Wittenberg, October 1517.

title: First Epistle of Clement
author: Clement of Rome
desc: A pastoral letter from the Roman church to Corinth on order, humility, and obedience — one of the earliest post-apostolic writings.

title: Letter to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna
desc: A letter to the church at Philippi on righteousness, avoiding heresy, and enduring persecution faithfully.

title: Martyrdom of Polycarp
author: Smyrnaean Church
desc: The earliest detailed account of Christian martyrdom — Polycarp's arrest, trial, and death at age 86.

title: Proslogion
author: Anselm of Canterbury
desc: The ontological argument for God's existence: "that than which nothing greater can be conceived" — faith seeking understanding.

title: Epistle of Barnabas
desc: An early letter reading the Old Testament typologically — the covenant, circumcision, and sabbath fulfilled in Christ.

title: Cur Deus Homo
author: Anselm of Canterbury
desc: "Why Did God Become Man?" — the foundational satisfaction theory of the Atonement, presented as a dialogue.

title: The Imitation of Christ
author: Thomas à Kempis
desc: The most-read Christian devotional after the Bible — four books on forsaking the world, following Christ inwardly, and the Eucharist.

title: Didache
desc: "The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles" — the earliest known manual of Christian ethics, baptism, Eucharist, and church order.

title: Epistle to Diognetus
desc: "What the soul is to the body, Christians are to the world" — an elegant early apology on the Christian way of life.

title: The Shepherd of Hermas
desc: Visions, Mandates, and Similitudes — an influential early Christian apocalypse on repentance and the church as a building under construction.

title: The Freedom of a Christian
author: Martin Luther
desc: "A Christian is a perfectly free lord of all, subject to none; a perfectly dutiful servant of all, subject to all."

title: Pilgrim's Progress
author: John Bunyan
desc: The great allegorical journey of Christian from the City of Destruction to the Celestial City.

title: Sinners in the Hands of an Angry God
author: Jonathan Edwards
desc: The most famous sermon of the Great Awakening — on God's wrath, sovereign mercy, and the urgency of repentance.
volume_label: Sinners in the Hands of an Angry God

title: Smalcald Articles
author: Martin Luther
desc: Luther's personal theological testament — the non-negotiables on justification and papal primacy, written for a council that never met.

title: Tome of Leo
author: Leo I of Rome
desc: Pope Leo's letter defining Christ's two natures — the theological foundation ratified at Chalcedon (451).

title: Schleitheim Confession
desc: Seven articles defining the Anabaptist movement: believers' baptism, the ban, the Lord's Supper, separation, shepherds, the sword, and the oath.

title: Dordrecht Confession
desc: Eighteen articles of the Dutch Mennonite tradition — baptism, the ban, foot-washing, non-resistance, and the oath.

title: Savoy Declaration
desc: The Congregationalist revision of the Westminster Confession — agreeing on doctrine while affirming the independence of local churches.

title: Small Catechism
author: Martin Luther
desc: Luther's brief instruction for household heads — the Ten Commandments, Creed, Lord's Prayer, Baptism, Confession, and the Lord's Supper.

title: Christianity and Liberalism
author: J. Gresham Machen
desc: The classic defence of historic Christianity against early twentieth-century theological liberalism — arguing they are two distinct religions.

title: Of the Mortification of Sin in Believers
author: John Owen
desc: Owen's Puritan masterpiece on killing sin — the Spirit as the sole agent of mortification, the danger of unmortified sin, and union with Christ as the root.

title: A Body of Divinity
author: Thomas Watson
desc: A clear and warm exposition of the Westminster Shorter Catechism — God's attributes, the covenant of grace, justification, sanctification, and glorification.

title: Five Theological Orations
author: Gregory of Nazianzus
desc: The five orations that earned Gregory the title 'The Theologian' — on who may do theology, the incomprehensibility of God, the Son's eternal generation, and the full deity of the Spirit.

title: Holiness
author: J. C. Ryle
desc: The Victorian bishop's thorough and searching treatment of sin, sanctification, the fight of faith, and practical godliness — deeply Puritan in spirit.

title: On the Holy Spirit
author: Basil of Caesarea
desc: Basil's defence of the Spirit's full deity — the doxological controversy, the Spirit as Lord and Life-giver, and the Spirit's indispensable role in sanctification and worship.

title: On Loving God
author: Bernard of Clairvaux
desc: Bernard's classic meditation on why God is to be loved without limit and the four ascending degrees of love — from self-love to loving even oneself only for God's sake.

title: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
author: John of Damascus
desc: The first systematic theology of Eastern Christianity — God and the Trinity, creation and human nature, the Incarnation, sacraments, and the defense of icons.

title: Selected Sermons
author: John Wesley
desc: Six of Wesley's Forty-Four Standard Sermons — salvation by faith, scriptural Christianity, the means of grace, and the catholic spirit of Christian unity.

title: All of Grace
author: Charles Spurgeon
desc: Spurgeon's most-read work — a plain-hearted appeal to sinners to receive the free grace of God in Christ, without money and without price.

title: Baltimore Catechism
desc: The standard American Catholic catechism for nearly a century — God, creation, the Creed, the sacraments, the commandments, and the last things in Q&A form.

title: Roman Catechism
desc: The Catechism of the Council of Trent — the definitive Counter-Reformation statement on the Creed, the sacraments, the commandments, and prayer.

title: Four Centuries on Love
author: Maximus the Confessor
desc: Four hundred chapters on the spiritual life as an ascent through love — detachment, prayer, the knowledge of God, and deification — drawn from Scripture and the Desert Fathers.

title: Triads in Defence of the Holy Hesychasts
author: Gregory Palamas
desc: The theological defence of hesychasm and the distinction between divine essence and energies — the uncreated light of Tabor is truly God, participable by the purified, though the divine essence remains forever beyond all creatures.

title: Discourses and Hymns of Divine Love
author: Symeon the New Theologian
desc: "The New Theologian" insists that the conscious experience of the Holy Spirit is the ordinary birthright of every Christian — with hymns of mystical experience unmatched in the patristic tradition.

title: Institutes of the Christian Religion
author: John Calvin
desc: The systematic masterwork of Reformed theology — knowledge of God and self, the law, the person and work of Christ, faith, justification, election, and the Church.

title: The Interior Castle
author: Teresa of Ávila
desc: Teresa's seven-mansioned castle of the soul — from self-knowledge and vocal prayer through the prayer of quiet and union to the spiritual marriage and Trinitarian indwelling.

title: Dark Night of the Soul
author: John of the Cross
desc: The poem and its commentary — active and passive purgations of sense and spirit, the nada way, and the transformation of the soul into God through the dark night of loving union.

title: Summa Theologica — Overview
author: Thomas Aquinas
desc: A guided tour of the Summa's four parts — God and creation, the last end and human acts, the virtues in detail, and the Incarnation with the sacraments.

title: Introduction to the Devout Life
author: Francis de Sales
desc: The gentle bishop's guide to sanctity in ordinary life — devotion for the married, the widowed, and the active, built on mental prayer, the sacraments, and the little virtues.

title: The Soul's Journey into God
author: Bonaventure
desc: Written on La Verna after Francis received the stigmata — six stages of contemplation ascending through creation, the soul, and being itself to the mystical darkness of union with the crucified Christ.

title: Humility
author: Andrew Murray
desc: Murray's searching study of the one virtue that underlies all others — Christ as the model of humility, and self-emptying as the path to union with God.

title: The Deeper Christian Life
author: Andrew Murray
desc: Murray's practical guide to Spirit-filled living — daily fellowship with God, the fullness of the Spirit, abiding in Christ, and the consecrated life of service.

title: Orthodoxy
author: G.K. Chesterton
desc: Chesterton's intellectual autobiography of faith — the argument from wonder, the paradoxes of Christianity, and why orthodoxy is the most adventurous idea in the world.

title: C.S. Lewis — Apologetics Overview
author: C.S. Lewis
desc: A guide to Lewis's major apologetic works — the trilemma, the argument from desire, Mere Christianity, The Problem of Pain, Miracles, and his enduring legacy.

title: Dietrich Bonhoeffer — Overview
author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer
desc: From the Berlin academy to Flossenbürg — cheap grace vs. costly grace, Life Together, the Confessing Church, and the theology emerging from prison.

title: A.W. Tozer — Overview
author: A.W. Tozer
desc: The prophetic pastor of Chicago — The Pursuit of God, The Knowledge of the Holy, his critique of entertainment Christianity, and his debt to the mystics.

title: B.B. Warfield — Overview
author: B.B. Warfield
desc: The last great voice of Old Princeton — the doctrine of biblical inspiration, concursive operation, the Plan of Salvation, and his critique of perfectionism.

title: The Everlasting Man
author: G.K. Chesterton
desc: Man as qualitatively different from animals, pagan myth as anticipation of Christ, and the Incarnation as the hinge of all history — the book that converted C.S. Lewis.

title: Lectures on Calvinism
author: Abraham Kuyper
desc: Calvinism as a comprehensive life-system, the doctrine of sphere sovereignty, and the Reformed vision for science, art, and the state — the Stone Lectures at Princeton.

title: Apologia Pro Vita Sua
author: John Henry Newman
desc: Newman's intellectual autobiography — the Oxford Movement, the Via Media, doctrinal development, and the role of conscience in his journey from Anglicanism to Rome.

title: Letter to the Romans
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: Ignatius's most passionate letter — written en route to martyrdom, pleading with Rome not to prevent his death so he may be "ground by the teeth of wild beasts" as wheat into bread for Christ.

title: Letter to the Philadelphians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: On unity around the one bishop, the danger of Judaizing schism, and the one Eucharist as the flesh of Christ and the one cup as his blood.

title: Letter to the Smyrnaeans
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: The strongest anti-Docetic letter — Christ truly suffered and rose bodily; without the bishop there is no valid Eucharist, baptism, or love-feast.

title: Letter to Polycarp
author: Ignatius of Antioch
desc: Personal counsel from a martyr-bishop to a younger bishop on pastoral vigilance, duties of the flock, marriage in the Lord, and the spiritual warfare of ministry.

title: Writings of Athenagoras
author: Athenagoras of Athens
desc: Two works: A Plea for the Christians (to Marcus Aurelius, refuting charges of atheism, incest, and cannibalism) and On the Resurrection of the Dead — the most philosophically polished early apology.

title: Dialogue with Trypho
author: Justin Martyr
desc: The longest surviving Christian apologetic dialogue — Justin debates a learned Jew on Christ as the fulfilment of Old Testament prophecy, the true circumcision, the two advents, and the calling of the Gentiles.

title: Against Heresies
author: Irenaeus of Lyons
desc: The foundational anti-Gnostic work in five books — exposing Valentinian and other Gnostic systems, then refuting them through the Rule of Faith, apostolic succession, and the unity of Scripture.

title: Apology
author: Tertullian
desc: The greatest Latin apology — addressed to the governors of the Roman provinces, defending Christians against all legal and social charges and arguing that Christianity is the true philosophy.

title: The Prescription Against Heretics
author: Tertullian
desc: Tertullian's brilliant juridical argument that heretics have no legal standing to use Scripture — the apostolic churches possess the faith; heretics are trespassers. Foundational for the concept of tradition.

title: Against Marcion — Books IV–V
author: Tertullian
desc: Tertullian's five-book refutation of Marcion's dualist gospel and Pauline canon. Book IV is the earliest extended Latin commentary on Luke; Book V defends Paul's epistles against Marcion's mutilations. Tr. Peter Holmes, ANF vol. 3 (1885).

title: Against Celsus
author: Origen
desc: Eight books answering Celsus's comprehensive attack on Christianity — the most extensive and systematic early Christian apologetic, covering history, philosophy, Scripture, miracles, and the nature of God.

title: On the Unity of the Church
author: Cyprian of Carthage
desc: "He cannot have God for his father who has not the Church for his mother" — the foundational treatise on ecclesiology, written during the Novatianist schism, arguing that the one Church is the body of Christ.
volume_label: On the Unity of the Church

title: On the Lapsed
author: Cyprian of Carthage
desc: On the pastoral handling of those who lapsed during the Decian persecution — repentance, discipline, and the mercy of the Church toward those who fell and wish to return.

title: On the Incarnation
author: Athanasius of Alexandria
desc: "He became what we are that He might make us what He is" — the young Athanasius's masterwork on why the Word became flesh, the nature of the Atonement, and the proof of the Resurrection. C.S. Lewis called it a masterpiece.

title: Life of Antony
author: Athanasius of Alexandria
desc: The biography of St. Antony of Egypt that launched Christian monasticism — his combat with demons, his counsel to monks, and his long life of desert contemplation as a mirror of the spiritual warfare every Christian faces.

title: Orations of Gregory of Nazianzus
author: Gregory of Nazianzus
desc: The complete surviving orations including the Five Theological Orations (27–31) on the incomprehensibility of God, the eternal generation of the Son, and the full deity of the Spirit — the works that earned him the title "The Theologian."

title: The Great Catechism
author: Gregory of Nyssa
desc: A systematic apologetic theology — the Trinity, creation and the fall, the Incarnation and Atonement, baptism, and the Eucharist — written as a manual for catechists converting pagans, Jews, and heretics.

title: Catechetical Lectures
author: Cyril of Jerusalem
desc: 24 lectures delivered to candidates for baptism — a systematic introduction to the faith through the Creed, Scripture, the sacraments, and the Holy Spirit. The most complete surviving example of 4th-century catechesis.

title: On the Duties of the Clergy
author: Ambrose of Milan
desc: Three books on clerical ethics modeled on Cicero's De Officiis but radically Christianized — the four cardinal virtues, true honor, charity as the highest good, and the example of the patriarchs and Christ.

title: The Commonitory
author: Vincent of Lerins
desc: The "Vincentian Canon" — that true Catholic faith is what has been believed "everywhere, always, and by all." A concise guide to distinguishing orthodox development of doctrine from heretical innovation.

title: The City of God
author: Augustine of Hippo
desc: 22 books written in the wake of Rome's sack — demolishing pagan religion, tracing the two cities (love of God vs. love of self) through all history, and culminating in the eternal Sabbath of the City of God.

title: On the Trinity
author: Augustine of Hippo
desc: 15 books — the first seven establishing Trinitarian doctrine from Scripture, the remaining eight exploring psychological analogies of the Trinity in memory, understanding, and will. Augustine's greatest speculative work.

title: On Christian Doctrine
author: Augustine of Hippo
desc: The foundational text of Christian hermeneutics — how to read Scripture, the distinction between signs and things, the primacy of love in interpretation, and the cultivation of wisdom and eloquence for preaching.

title: Enchiridion (Faith, Hope, and Love)
author: Augustine of Hippo
desc: "What should be believed, what hoped for, and what loved" — Augustine's compact handbook of Christian doctrine organized around the three theological virtues: the Creed (faith), the Lord's Prayer (hope), and the two great commandments (love).

title: Ecclesiastical History
author: Eusebius of Caesarea
desc: 10 books tracing the history of the Church from the apostles to Constantine — the succession of bishops, the martyrs, the heresies refuted, and the providential triumph of Christianity. The founding document of church history.

title: The Conferences
author: John Cassian
desc: 24 recorded conversations with the Egyptian desert fathers on discretion, prayer, the eight deadly thoughts, friendship, and grace — the foundational text of Western monastic spirituality, deeply formative for Benedict of Nursia.

title: Sermons of Leo the Great
author: Leo I of Rome
desc: 96 sermons on the Incarnation, the Lord's Prayer, Lent, the Passion, and Easter — a treasury of patristic preaching that shaped the Western liturgical year and the theology of Christ's two natures.

title: The Book of Pastoral Rule
author: Gregory the Great
desc: Four parts on who should undertake pastoral office, the inner life of the shepherd, how to address the different conditions of hearers, and how to return to oneself after engagement with the world. Alfred the Great called it essential reading.

title: The Consolation of Philosophy
author: Boethius
desc: Written in prison awaiting execution — Lady Philosophy visits Boethius, argues against Fortune's wheel, and leads him through providence, free will, and the highest good to the vision of God as the true source of all happiness. The most read philosophical text of the Middle Ages.

title: The Practice of the Presence of God
author: Brother Lawrence
desc: Letters and conversations of a humble lay brother in a Paris monastery on cultivating unbroken awareness of God's presence in the midst of kitchen work and daily life. One of the most beloved short spiritual classics.

title: The Holy War
author: John Bunyan
desc: The allegory of Mansoul — its creation, fall to Diabolus, siege and redemption by Emmanuel, and the ongoing war for its streets and gates. Bunyan's second great allegory, often considered his most theological.

title: Grace Abounding to the Chief of Sinners
author: John Bunyan
desc: Bunyan's spiritual autobiography — years of spiritual torment, Satanic temptations, wrestling with Scripture, and the final assurance of grace "abounding" to the chief of sinners. The direct source from which Pilgrim's Progress grew.

title: Institutes — Book I: Knowledge of God the Creator
author: John Calvin
desc: Book I of Calvin's Institutes — the knowledge of God and of ourselves, Scripture as the spectacles of faith, the Trinity, creation, providence, and angels. The opening of the most comprehensive work of Reformed theology.

title: Institutes of the Christian Religion — Vol. I (Books I–II)
author: John Calvin
desc: Books I–II of the Institutes — the knowledge of God the Creator and Redeemer. The Trinity, Scripture, the fall, the law, the Mosaic economy, and the two natures of Christ.

title: Institutes of the Christian Religion — Vol. II (Books III–IV)
author: John Calvin
desc: Books III–IV of the Institutes — the way we receive Christ's grace (faith, justification, sanctification, election) and the external means by which God invites us into the society of Christ (the Church, ministry, sacraments, civil government).

title: Fox's Book of Martyrs
author: John Foxe
desc: The great Protestant martyrology — from the early church through the Marian persecution in England, recording the sufferings of those who died for the Protestant faith. Hugely formative for Anglophone Protestant identity.

title: The Ministry of Intercession
author: Andrew Murray
desc: Murray's call to the church to recover the ministry of intercession — why the Church fails to pray, how to pray with power, and how every believer can become a channel of God's grace to the world through persistent intercession.

title: Lord, Teach Us to Pray
author: Andrew Murray
desc: Thirty-one meditations on the Lord's Prayer and the life of prayer — working through each petition to show how the Lord's Prayer is both a model of prayer and a school of the entire Christian life.

title: A Body of Divinity — Vol. I
author: Thomas Watson
volume_label: A Body of Divinity — Vol. I
desc: Watson's warm and clear exposition of the Westminster Shorter Catechism — God's nature and attributes, creation, the covenant of grace, sin, and the person of Christ.

title: Summa Theologica — Part I (Prima Pars)
author: Thomas Aquinas
desc: Questions 1–119 — Sacred doctrine, the existence and nature of God, the Trinity, creation, angels, human nature, and divine governance.

title: Summa Theologica — Part I–II (Prima Secundae)
author: Thomas Aquinas
desc: Questions 1–114 — The last end of man, human acts, the passions, habits and virtue, law (eternal, natural, human, divine), and the grace of God.

title: Summa Theologica — Part II–II (Secunda Secundae)
author: Thomas Aquinas
desc: Questions 1–189 — The three theological virtues (faith, hope, charity) and the four cardinal virtues (prudence, justice, fortitude, temperance), each examined alongside its opposing vices.

title: Summa Theologica — Part III (Tertia Pars)
author: Thomas Aquinas
desc: Questions 1–90 plus the Supplement — The Incarnation, life of Christ, the sacraments, and the last things. Left incomplete at Aquinas's death.

title: On First Principles (De Principiis)
author: Origen
desc: The first systematic Christian theology — four books on God and the Trinity, the soul and the world, free will and redemption, and the spiritual interpretation of Scripture.

title: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles
author: John Chrysostom
desc: 55 homilies on the missionary expansion of the Church — rich in practical exhortation on prayer, almsgiving, unity, and bold witness to the resurrection.

title: Religious Affections
author: Jonathan Edwards
desc: Edwards's masterwork on the nature of genuine religion — twelve unreliable signs vs. the twelve reliable marks of truly gracious and holy affections.

title: Freedom of the Will
author: Jonathan Edwards
desc: The most rigorous philosophical defence of Calvinist determinism in English — arguing that moral necessity is compatible with freedom and that God's sovereignty over the will is essential to all true virtue.

title: Writings of Francis of Assisi
author: Francis of Assisi
desc: The Canticle of the Sun, selected Admonitions, and the Testament — the personal writings of the Poverello: praise, poverty, and the love of all creatures.

title: Revelations of Divine Love
author: Julian of Norwich
desc: "All shall be well" — 86 chapters of mystical theology, the first book written in English by a woman: divine love, the hazelnut, the lord and the servant, and the motherhood of God.

title: Spiritual Exercises
author: Ignatius of Loyola
desc: The four-week retreat program that formed the Jesuits — meditation on sin, the life of Christ, election, and the Contemplation to Attain Love.

title: A Serious Call to a Devout and Holy Life
author: William Law
desc: The book that converted John Wesley and moved C.S. Lewis — bringing every hour and occupation under the rule of devotion to God.

title: The Bondage of the Will
author: Martin Luther
desc: Luther's reply to Erasmus: apart from grace the will is bound to sin; salvation is entirely of God. The book Luther himself considered his most important.

title: Commentary on Galatians
author: Martin Luther
desc: Luther’s verse-by-verse exposition of Galatians — his most-read commentary, where he identifies Paul’s doctrine of justification by faith alone as the heart of the Gospel.

title: Selected Sermons
author: George Whitefield
desc: Five sermons from the preacher of the Great Awakening — imputed righteousness, the new birth, walking with God, and the danger of almost believing.

title: The Reformed Pastor
author: Richard Baxter
desc: The Puritan classic on pastoral ministry — overseeing yourself before your flock; diligent catechizing and personal visitation as the heart of the pastor's work.

title: The Rule of St. Benedict
author: Benedict of Nursia
desc: The foundational rule of Western monasticism — governing prayer, work, humility, and community life with extraordinary wisdom. Followed continuously for 1,500 years.

title: Works of Pseudo-Dionysius
author: Pseudo-Dionysius the Areopagite
desc: The Celestial Hierarchy, Divine Names, and Ecclesiastical Hierarchy — foundational for all Christian mysticism East and West, profoundly shaping Aquinas, Eckhart, and hesychasm.

title: The Cloud of Unknowing
desc: Anonymous 14th-century English apophatic mysticism — contemplating God not through ideas but through love alone, in the cloud where all thought must cease.

title: The Dialogue
author: Catherine of Siena
desc: Dictated in ecstasy — Catherine's dialogue with God the Father on discretion, prayer, the Three Stairs of the soul, and divine providence. Doctor of the Church.

title: Sermons
author: Meister Eckhart
desc: German Dominican sermons on the birth of the Word in the soul, detachment, and the ground of being — the most radical and influential of the medieval German mystics.

title: Holy Living
author: Jeremy Taylor
desc: The greatest Anglican devotional classic — means of holy living, sobriety, justice, and religion in daily life, with rules and prayers for ordinary Christian existence.
